prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde was performed using **DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier `standard`) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the parent molecule, with cation and anion states (charges +1 and −1, both multiplicity 2) used to compute f⁺ and f⁻. The input geometry was user-supplied and not re-optimized at this level. Mulliken partial charges were used to compute the Fukui indices. All SCF calculations converged and charge-conservation checks passed (∑f⁺ = 1.0000, ∑f⁻ = 1.0000). The most electrophilic atom is **carbon at index 2** (1-based label 3, aromatic ortho carbon relative to the aldehyde), with **f⁺ = 0.39938968**. The most nucleophilic atom is the **carbonyl oxygen** (atom 0, 1-based label 1), with **f⁻ = 0.23839906**. The integrity status is `trustworthy: true`, with all validation checks passed. Full atom-resolved Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptors are available in the result block above.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">14
